figures.caption: Figure 1. Self-Swap Guidance (SSG) generates higher-fidelity images over a wider range of guidance scale. In contrast, existing methods [1, 18, 19] suffer from poor details at lower guidance scale, or noise, oversaturation, and oversimplified details at higher guidance scale.
Figure 2. Visualisations of guidance patterns and the iteratively denoised images across different timesteps. The text prompt used is “A loft bed with a dresser underneath it”.
Figure 4. Qualitative comparison of conditional image generation by SDXL.
